context.other_context: Consulted synthesis/PROMPT.md, schemas/synthesis-output.schema.json, schemas/synthesis-item.schema.json, manifest/2026-07-18.md, the dated capture-file inventory, and git status. Synthesis A existed on GitHub but was not present in the stale local checkout during synthesis and was not read; it was fetched only afterward for publication.

items.claim: AI compute demand is moving from a technology-sector capex story into explicit grid, transmission, and advanced-reactor policy and deployment decisions.
specifics: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission Docket RD26-7-000
U.S. Department of Energy 2026 National Transmission Needs Study
items.falsifier: FERC and DOE remove computational-load language from final actions, transmission priorities do not shift toward compute clusters, and announced reactor projects cease tying deployment to data-center demand.
items.horizon: 7-30 days for regulatory and procurement follow-through; 1-3 years for physical deployment
items.flag_hit: Government funding and policy are quietly moving toward AI power infrastructure, while major tech financial data diverges from the public AI-investment narrative.

items.claim: Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 release is increasing price and margin pressure on Western frontier-model providers while challenging their lead on coding benchmarks.
items.falsifier: Independent benchmark replications place Kimi K3 materially below the named Western models, its promised open weights do not ship, and API prices among frontier providers do not respond.
items.flag_hit: A non-Western AI model is outperforming Western models on reported benchmarks, and unrelated labs are converging on open-weight efficiency architectures.

items.claim: China is pairing frontier-model and domestic-compute releases with international AI governance initiatives to compete on standards as well as capability.
specifics: China Ministry of Industry and Information Technology
items.falsifier: The announced international body and action plans fail to gain operating members, programs, standards adoption, or follow-on implementation outside China.
items.horizon: 14-30 days for organizational follow-through; 6-18 months for standards uptake
items.flag_hit: Government bodies are quietly moving on AI regulation and Chinese government AI investment suggests strategic economic application.

items.claim: Renewed U.S.-Iran hostilities are transmitting through oil, LNG, port operations, freight risk pricing, and near-term inflation expectations rather than remaining a contained military event.
items.falsifier: A durable ceasefire restores normal Gulf port and tanker operations, war-risk and bunker surcharges reverse, and oil and Asian LNG benchmarks return to pre-escalation ranges.
items.horizon: 7 days for shipping and commodity confirmation; 14-30 days for inflation pass-through
items.flag_hit: Commodity prices and logistics movements are consistent with foreknowledge or expectation of geopolitical escalation, and port rerouting is appearing across locations.

items.claim: The Aktau Container Hub and its integrated China-Kazakhstan terminal network are turning the Middle Corridor into an operational alternative to Suez- and Russia-dependent Eurasian routes.
items.falsifier: The hub fails to sustain scheduled throughput, border and Caspian transfers keep end-to-end transit uncompetitive, or the integration agreement does not produce shared operations across the three named hubs.
items.horizon: 30-90 days for repeat throughput and schedule reliability
items.flag_hit: Belt and Road port and rail infrastructure is creating a strategic alternative to Western-controlled shipping lanes and traditional chokepoints.

items.claim: Asian and BRICS payment policy is moving toward bank- and central-bank-controlled interoperable digital rails rather than the issuer-led stablecoin model dominant in U.S. debate.
items.falsifier: The reported September launches or linkages are delayed without replacement dates, cross-border transaction volumes stall, and participating central banks revert to non-interoperable domestic pilots.
items.horizon: 14-30 days for launch preparation; by September 2026 for named rollout milestones
items.flag_hit: The digital yuan or mBridge is expanding while Western attention is on crypto markets, Hong Kong is serving as a policy test bed, and BRICS nations are coordinating payment infrastructure outside SWIFT.

items.claim: The Eastern capture set contains irreconcilable China growth and same-day Hang Seng figures, so it does not support a single quantitative conclusion about current Chinese growth or market direction.
specifics: National Bureau of Statistics of China
items.falsifier: The underlying releases and exchange closes reconcile the quoted figures as different, correctly labeled periods or sessions with no remaining conflict.
items.horizon: Immediate; resolvable against authoritative releases and exchange closes
items.flag_hit: Chinese economic data and Asian market movements were requested as divergence flags, but the two Eastern answers themselves diverge.

items.claim: Eastern collection degraded selectively: Kimi K3 produced two unavailable AI attempts before K2.6 refused, DeepSeek refused geopolitics twice, and isolated Kimi stocks and DeepSeek companies attempts were unavailable before successful retries.
items.falsifier: A repeat collection with the same prompts and model versions completes substantively without retries or refusals across the affected domains.

items.claim: No Western markets capture substantiated unusual insider buying in the collection window despite prompts designed to surface insider or filing anomalies.
items.falsifier: A capture from the window identifies a named, material insider purchase backed by a current filing.
items.flag_hit: Unusual insider filing activity or insider buys; no hit returned.

items.claim: Asian consumers are adopting social, quick, and agent-mediated commerce faster than Western consumers while shifting loyalty toward locally relevant brands and utility-based value.
items.falsifier: Independent transaction data show social, quick, and agentic commerce adoption flattening in Asia, Western adoption closing the gap, and local-brand share reversing.
items.horizon: 30-90 days for merchant and brand allocation changes; 12-24 months for Western diffusion
items.flag_hit: Asian consumer behavior is diverging from Western behavior, non-Western generational shifts may lead Western markets, and local values are changing global brand loyalty.

items.claim: Tariff-deadline front-loading is raising freight volumes and rates despite softer underlying orders, creating a hidden inventory build and a risk of a late-Q3 freight demand cliff.
specifics: U.S. Department of Transportation Bureau of Transportation Statistics
items.falsifier: Inventory-to-sales ratios do not rise after the import surge, bookings and spot rates remain firm after the tariff deadlines, and final demand absorbs the added stock without a Q3 volume decline.
items.flag_hit: Freight rates are diverging from manufacturing and order data in a pattern consistent with hidden inventory buildup.

items.claim: Social retrieval found no urgent new logistics disruption while institutional and research captures reported multi-hub congestion, Gulf suspension, and U.S. capacity tightening, revealing a source-visibility gap rather than a resolved supply picture.
specifics: U.S. DOT Bureau of Transportation Statistics
items.falsifier: Port and DOT operational data normalize or the named social accounts begin reporting the same disruptions with comparable urgency and specificity.
items.flag_hit: Port congestion or rerouting is appearing at multiple unconnected locations simultaneously.

items.claim: Critical-mineral risk is shifting from headline ore prices toward concentrated refining control, export licensing, and sulphuric-acid processing costs that can delay downstream supply even when metal spot prices look soft.
items.falsifier: Export licenses clear predictably, sulphur and acid costs normalize without project delays, and refined critical-mineral output grows fast enough to prevent downstream shortages.
items.flag_hit: Chinese rare-earth export policy is quietly shifting while critical-mineral supply-chain data diverges from commodity price signals.

items.claim: Fusion commercialization is advancing through industrial bottlenecks—repeatable drivers, target manufacturing, tritium-material computation, diagnostics, and public-market funding—rather than through a single plasma breakthrough.
specifics: Hefei Institutes of Physical Science ITER IDFI project
items.falsifier: The named driver, target-factory, diagnostics, and materials programs fail their next published milestones or cannot demonstrate repeatability and manufacturability outside one-off tests.
items.horizon: 30-90 days for milestone disclosures; multi-year for power deployment
items.flag_hit: Fusion funding and government infrastructure are shifting, and Chinese fusion infrastructure has reached an under-covered milestone.

items.claim: Persistent net migration into selected Southern and Mountain West corridors alongside contracting multifamily permits is setting up localized shelter-cost pressure despite softer prices in some oversupplied Sun Belt markets.
items.falsifier: Permits and starts recover before vacancy tightens, migration stalls or reverses, or existing excess inventory keeps rents and shelter inflation flat in the named corridors.
items.horizon: 30-90 days for leading indicators; 18-24 months for the projected supply gap
items.flag_hit: Housing permit data is diverging from population movement data in ways that could create future supply gaps.
